task.title: Book My Show: Broken Refund
task.description: The ticketing system has a flaw in its cancellation logic. Currently, users can cancel tickets even after a movie has started. Additionally, while the system issues a refund, it fails to release the seat back to the available inventory. You must fix both issues.
constraints: Ensure seat is released back to inventory
Prevent cancellation after movie start time

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Validation of movie start time and seat status

expectedApproaches.tradeoff: Ensures atomicity between refund and seat release

followUpIntent.hint: Ask how to handle cases where the user's loyalty points calculation is more complex

task.title: Book My Show: Webhook Idempotency
task.description: Our payment gateway occasionally sends duplicate webhook notifications due to network retries. The system currently processes every notification it receives, leading to duplicate loyalty points being awarded to users. You must implement idempotency to ensure each payment event is processed exactly once.
constraints: Ensure no duplicate loyalty points are awarded

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Handling unique constraint violations in the database

expectedApproaches.tradeoff: Reliable and simple, avoids race conditions at the DB level

followUpIntent.hint: Ask about scaling the idempotency table for millions of events

task.title: Book My Show: Cache Stampede
task.description: Under heavy peak load, the system experiences a 'Cache Stampede' when the movie details cache expires. Thousands of requests hit the database simultaneously to refresh the same data. You must implement a 'Single-flight' pattern using a Mutex to protect the database.
constraints: Only one request should hit the database for the same showId during cache refresh
Maintain low latency for concurrent requests

interviewerFocusAreas.scope: Proper use of Mutex and double-checked locking

expectedApproaches.tradeoff: Slightly increases code complexity but provides high protection for the DB

followUpIntent.hint: Ask about distributed locking if we had multiple backend instances
